sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: 51Talk full year 2025 financial and operational results
feed.summary: 51Talk Online Education Group (NYSE:COE) announced strong financial results for the full year ended December 31, 2025, with net revenues surging by 88.6% to $95.6 million, driven by its international business expansion. Active students increased by 79.3%, reaching approximately 170.3 thousand, reflecting robust demand in Southeast Asia and other emerging markets. While the company achieved a healthy operating cash inflow, increased sales and marketing expenses and investments in its AI platform led to widened operating and net losses for the year.

feed.title: Fastenal stock consolidates as team gears up for Martinsville race
feed.summary: Fastenal (FAST) stock is consolidating near recent highs, trading between $44.70 and $45.30, exhibiting a balance of bullish and bearish momentum. The company announced its sponsorship for Ryan Preece and the No. 60 Body Guard Ford team at Martinsville. Technical indicators suggest short-to-medium-term selling pressure, but the long-term trend remains strong, with a high probability of price increase in the coming week.

feed.title: Vanguard disaggregates holdings; Waters Corp shows 0% stake (WAT)
feed.summary: Vanguard Group has filed an Amendment No. 16 to Schedule 13G/A, reporting 0% beneficial ownership and 0 shares in Waters Corp (WAT). This change is attributed to an internal realignment on January 12, 2026, where certain Vanguard subsidiaries will now report their holdings separately, referencing SEC Release No. 34-39538. The filing indicates a disaggregation of reporting rather than a sale of shares.

feed.title: Multimillion-dollar paycheck: Here's how much Norwegian's new CEO could make
feed.summary: Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings' new CEO, John Chidsey, is set to receive a multimillion-dollar compensation package. His pay is tied to the company's long-term performance, aligning with ongoing governance changes at the Miami-based cruise operator.

feed.title: Consumers Energy making upgrades to electric grid
feed.summary: Consumers Energy's Reliability Action Plan has been approved after a year-long process, allowing for significant upgrades to Michigan's electric grid. This investment aims to provide more reliable power to approximately 2 million homes and businesses, particularly during severe weather, and follows recent efforts to recover from an ice storm. The company stated that these upgrades have already reduced average outage times and will continue to improve grid resilience while helping customers manage their energy bills.

feed.title: Trump wants Deere, Irving-based Caterpillar to cut farmers’ tractor costs
feed.summary: Former President Donald Trump has called on major agricultural supply companies, including John Deere, Case (CNH), and Irving-based Caterpillar, to reduce the costs of tractors and equipment for farmers. Following his statement, shares of Deere & Co fell by 2% and CNH by 1%, while Caterpillar remained largely unchanged. Trump also announced measures to support agricultural communities, such as new loan guarantees from the SBA for farmers and food suppliers, and an update to renewable fuel standards.

feed.title: Colgate-Palmolive: One of Corporate America’s Last Remaining DEI Holdouts
feed.summary: The National Legal and Policy Center (NLPC) is calling out Colgate-Palmolive as one of the few remaining corporations that has not rolled back its Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) board mandates. NLPC has sponsored Proposal 4 for Colgate's May 8, 2026 Annual Meeting, urging the company to eliminate identity-based criteria for selecting directors, citing legal risks and deteriorating financial performance under its current board. The article highlights that many other major corporations, some after negotiations with NLPC, have already removed discriminatory language from their board selection processes.

feed.title: Jim Cramer on Paychex: “This is a stock that strangely has been crushed by AI disruption worries, I think they’re overblown”
feed.summary: Jim Cramer discussed Paychex, stating that the stock has been unduly impacted by fears of AI disruption. He believes these concerns are exaggerated and that the market has unfairly punished Paychex. This suggests Cramer sees potential value in the company despite the prevailing anxiety about AI's influence.

feed.title: Oracle’s Credit Risk Measure Nears Record High on AI-Debt Fears
feed.summary: Oracle's credit risk measure is approaching an all-time closing high, signaling investor concern over the company's substantial debt. This rise is attributed to worries about AI-related debt, alongside increasing oil prices and declining stock prices. The cost of protecting Oracle's debt against default has surged to 198.58 basis points, nearing its previous peak from December 2008.

feed.title: Jim Cramer breaks down the data center growth story for Generac Holdings
feed.summary: Jim Cramer highlighted Generac Holdings' potential for growth, specifically linking it to the expanding data center industry. He suggests that the company's products are becoming increasingly essential as data centers proliferate and require more robust power solutions. This connection to the critical infrastructure of the digital economy positions Generac for significant future expansion.

feed.title: Vanguard disaggregates holdings after realignment; WFC (NYSE: WFC) shows 0 ownership
feed.summary: Vanguard has filed an amendment to its Schedule 13G/A, indicating zero beneficial ownership in Wells Fargo & Co (WFC) following an internal realignment effective January 12, 2026. This change means that certain Vanguard subsidiaries will now report their holdings separately, in reliance on SEC Release No. 34-39538. The filing clarifies that this is a procedural reporting change rather than a trading event, with subsequent filings from subsidiaries expected to show any position changes.

feed.title: Vanguard disaggregates holdings; Zimmer Biomet (ZBH) shows 0 shares
feed.summary: The Vanguard Group has filed an Amendment No. 9 to a Schedule 13G/A for Zimmer Biomet Holdings Inc (ZBH), reporting 0 shares and 0% beneficial ownership. This change is due to an internal realignment effective January 12, 2026, allowing certain Vanguard subsidiaries to report their holdings separately, as per SEC Release No. 34-39538, rather than indicating a sale of shares. The filing is administrative, clarifying reporting procedures and beneficial ownership attribution following the realignment.

feed.title: Vanguard amends Schedule 13G for Xcel Energy (NASDAQ: XEL)
feed.summary: The Vanguard Group has filed an Amendment No. 11 to a Schedule 13G/A for Xcel Energy (NASDAQ: XEL), reporting 0 shares beneficially owned, representing 0% of the class. This change is due to an internal realignment effective January 12, 2026, where certain Vanguard subsidiaries will now report their holdings separately in reliance on SEC Release No. 34-39538. Ashley Grim, Head of Global Fund Administration, signed the filing dated March 27, 2026.

feed.title: Eversource Takes a Hit as Regulators Reset ROE and Trigger Refund Risk
feed.summary: Eversource Energy (ES) is facing a significant hit as a new FERC ruling resets the New England Transmission Owners' base Return on Equity (ROE) to 9.57%, leading to a 100 basis point drag in 2026 and potential refund obligations. Despite this headwind and a recent stock pullback of 11.51%, Bank of America maintains a "Buy" rating with a $73 price target, citing Eversource's substantial capital plan of $26.5 billion through 2030, projected rate base growth, and a multi-decade dividend growth streak (currently yielding 4.49%). The company's pivot to a pure-play regulated utility status and long-term EPS growth guidance of 5% to 7% are expected to support its long-term investment appeal for retirement investors.

feed.title: Vanguard (VMC) disaggregates holdings; parent reports zero VMC ownership
feed.summary: The Vanguard Group has filed an amended Schedule 13G/A report for Vulcan Materials Co (VMC), indicating zero beneficial ownership of VMC common stock. This change is a result of an internal realignment, where Vanguard's subsidiaries will now report beneficial ownership separately, consistent with SEC Release No. 34-39538. The filing clarifies that this is an administrative reallocation of reporting responsibility and not a market transaction involving the sale of VMC shares.

feed.title: Steel Dynamics Announces Retirement of Two Long-Tenured Directors
feed.summary: Steel Dynamics (STLD) announced that co-founder Richard Teets, Jr. and director Gabriel Shaheen will retire from the board after the May 6, 2026 annual meeting, reducing the board size from nine to seven members. The company emphasized that their departures are amicable and not due to any disagreements. Teets was highlighted as a key architect of the company's growth, while Shaheen was praised for his financial acumen.

feed.title: Why F5 (FFIV) Stock Is Down Today
feed.summary: Shares of F5 (FFIV) fell 4.2% today after its CEO sold over $1 million worth of company stock, a move that concerned investors despite being part of a pre-arranged trading plan. This decline also coincided with broader technology sector weakness driven by rising Treasury yields and stagflation fears. The recent sale, however, follows positive news two days prior regarding F5's collaborations with Forcepoint and NVIDIA to strengthen its position in the AI market, indicating the company's strategic growth in the sector.

feed.title: Assessing Deere (DE) Valuation After Strong Investor Response To New Tarter Manufacturing Collaboration
feed.summary: Deere (DE) is gaining investor attention following its new manufacturing collaboration with Tarter USA for Flex Wing Rotary Cutters under the Frontier brand. The stock has shown positive momentum with a 23.9% return in three months and is trading at a slight discount to fair value estimates, driven by a "replacement cycle" narrative focused on precision technology adoption by farmers. However, the company's future profitability hinges on farmer adoption of high-tech equipment and managing potential warranty or production cost issues.

feed.title: J.M. Smucker Launches New Look for Smucker’s Fruit Spreads
feed.summary: The J.M. Smucker Company has unveiled a new packaging design for its Smucker's fruit spreads, marking the first redesign in nearly 30 years. The refresh aims to merge the brand's established heritage with contemporary snacking trends while retaining the well-known gingham print and quality of its jams. The new jars will feature amplified fruit imagery and distinct, vibrant colors, beginning their rollout this spring.

feed.title: Jim Cramer on BXP, Inc.: “I Just Don’t Want It”
feed.summary: Jim Cramer expressed disinterest in BXP, Inc. (NYSE:BXP), citing structural issues with office REITs, a risky 5% yield, and higher energy costs affecting the market. He believes that for such risks, a yield of at least 6.5% would be necessary. Cramer had previously noted BXP's dividend trim for growth projects but pointed out that the stock has since declined over 30%.

feed.title: biote (BTMD) Balance Sheet & Financial Health Metrics
feed.summary: This article provides an overview of biote (BTMD)'s financial health and balance sheet metrics. It highlights key figures such as a negative shareholder equity of $-50.4M and total debt of $107.0M, resulting in a debt-to-equity ratio of -212.4%. The company's EBIT is $35.1M, with an interest coverage ratio of 3.2, and it holds $24.1M in cash and short-term investments.

feed.title: Jim Cramer on Cardinal Health: “I Want You to Buy It and Buy It, Get This, Aggressively”
feed.summary: Jim Cramer strongly endorsed Cardinal Health (NYSE: CAH), advising investors to buy it aggressively despite recent dips. He praised CEO Jason Hollar's performance and the company's strategic acquisitions, even suggesting the stock could reach $300. While acknowledging CAH's potential, the article also promotes alternative AI stocks with higher perceived upside.

feed.title: CAMX Stock Price, News & Analysis
feed.summary: Camurus AB (CAMX) is a biopharmaceutical company specializing in long-acting medicines using its proprietary FluidCrystal® technology for severe and chronic diseases like opioid dependence, acromegaly, and polycystic liver disease. The article details its key products Buvidal/Brixadi and CAM2029 (Oczyesa/Oclaiz), clinical development, and collaborations with partners like Eli Lilly. It also provides stock information, news updates, financial highlights, and upcoming regulatory events for CAMX.

feed.title: T‑Mobile's Hometown Grants Top $21.5M, Empowering 475 Small Towns Nationwide
feed.summary: T-Mobile has announced the latest 25 recipients of its Hometown Grants program, bringing the total investment to over $21.5 million across 475 small towns and rural communities in 49 states and Puerto Rico. The program, which started in April 2021, has impacted 2.8 million people and created nearly 2,700 jobs through projects such as STEM initiatives, revitalizing historic buildings, and creating community hubs. With only one more round of grants remaining, T-Mobile is nearing its goal of empowering 500 towns, collaborating with Main Street America to make impactful local visions a reality.

feed.title: Investment Advisor Firm Adopts Customer-First Approach to Managed Services
feed.summary: An independent wealth management and financial planning firm experienced rapid growth but faced IT challenges, including high latency in legacy applications and inefficient IT support. They partnered with CBIZ, selecting them due to their detailed discovery process, SOC 2 audit certification, and tailored IT solution. This collaboration led to enhanced IT operations, improved user experience, streamlined workstation and device management, optimized file management, centralized password and application management, and ongoing security enhancements.

feed.title: Cross Timbers: Q4 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Cross Timbers Royalty Trust reported a profit of $1.4 million, or 23 cents per share, in its fourth quarter, with revenues of $1.6 million. For the full year, the company's profit reached $4.5 million, or 75 cents per share, on revenues of $5.8 million. This financial data was generated by Automated Insights using information from Zacks Investment Research.

feed.title: Vanguard (VRTX) parent reports 0 shares; subsidiaries to report holdings separately

This change means that certain Vanguard subsidiaries will now report their beneficial ownership separately, relying on SEC Release No. 34-39538. The filing explicitly states that the parent company no longer has beneficial ownership over securities held by these disaggregated subsidiaries, and subsequent filings from affiliates are expected to show their individual holdings.

feed.title: SCE Extends More Than 1,000 Offers to Community Members for Eaton Fire Recovery
feed.summary: Southern California Edison (SCE) has reached a significant milestone in its Wildfire Recovery Compensation Program, extending over 1,000 offers totaling nearly $380 million to individuals and businesses affected by the Eaton Fire. The program aims to provide quick and compassionate support, with a streamlined process for claims and payments. Over 400 claimants have already received payments, demonstrating the program's efficiency in aiding recovery efforts.

feed.title: Align Technology, QuidelOrtho, DexCom, Insulet, and Omnicell Shares Are Falling, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Several major stock indices, including the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average, declined today due to escalating uncertainty surrounding the U.S.-Iran conflict and Trump administration policy deadlines, causing significant drops in various company shares. Specifically, Align Technology, QuidelOrtho, DexCom, Insulet, and Omnicell experienced notable share price decreases. The market's negative reaction to unpredictability was also reflected in a dip in consumer confidence, though such price drops can sometimes present buying opportunities for high-quality stocks.

feed.title: Consumers Energy residential electric bills to increase by 6.1 percent in May
feed.summary: The Michigan Public Service Commission has approved a 6.1% electricity rate hike for Consumers Energy, effective May 1. This will add an average of $6.46 to residential monthly bills for typical customers. The increase, which is 40% lower than Consumers Energy's initial request, is intended to support improvements in the utility's power grid reliability.
